name: A Red Chili Nightmare
recipeIngredient: 12 Red chiles, whole and dried or fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the pinto beans in a bowl and add 2 to 3 cups of water. Soak overnight, checking occasionally and adding water as needed to keep them moist.
2. Transfer the beans and soaking water into a heavy saucepan. Add another 2 to 3 cups of water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for about 45 minutes until cooked but still firm. Drain the beans, reserving the cooking liquid.
3. Melt lard in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the drained beans and lightly fry them. Set aside.
4. Melt bacon drippings in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until translucent.
5. Combine sausage, beef, spices (through oregano), and 1/2 cup of reserved bean-cooking liquid in the pot with onion. Break up any lumps with a fork and cook, stirring occasionally until meat is very well browned.
6. Stir in all remaining ingredients into the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for an additional half hour, stirring occasionally. Add water only if needed to maintain a chunky soup consistency.
7. Taste when curiosity becomes unbearable and courage are strong. Adjust seasonings as desired.
